commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=982db6bc492ca000b90e2f73c3d6d992b1329723)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Discord voice Opus decode/encode call sites in `extensions/discord/src/voice/audio.ts` to use `libopus-wasm` `decode`/`encode` methods with options objects.

PR surface: Source +17. Total +17 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and dependency contracts: PR head calls `decoder.decode` and `encoder.encode`, while the declared published `libopus-wasm@0.0.1` API exposes `decodeFrame` and `encodePcm16`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The PR is protected by the `maintainer` label and blocked on an external dependency release plus package/lockfile validation, so it should stay human-owned rather than enter an automated repair lane.

target.title: Update Discord voice for redesigned libopus-wasm API
target.body:
## Summary

- Updates Discord voice receive and playback code to call the redesigned `libopus-wasm` API: `decode(packet, { maxFrameSize })` and `encode(pcm, { frameSize })`.
- Preserves Discord's 48 kHz stereo 20 ms frame sizing and PCM buffer conversion behavior.
- Leaves the dependency version unchanged for now because the matching `libopus-wasm` npm release is intentionally pending maintainer review.

## Pending Before Merge

- Publish the new `libopus-wasm` release that contains openclaw/libopus-wasm@7584d4f.
- Update `extensions/discord/package.json`, `extensions/discord/npm-shrinkwrap.json`, and root lockfile to that released version.
- Rerun the Discord voice test lane against the published dependency.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Runtime smoke with a temporary Node loader mapping `libopus-wasm` to local built `openclaw/libopus-wasm@7584d4f`: encoded one 960-frame Discord PCM buffer and decoded it back to 3840 bytes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord voice callers now use the redesigned `libopus-wasm` encode/decode methods.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with local built `openclaw/libopus-wasm@7584d4f` loaded via temporary Node loader.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Imported `extensions/discord/src/voice/audio.ts`, created a Discord Opus encode stream, encoded one 48 kHz stereo 20 ms PCM frame, then decoded the emitted packet with `decodeOpusStream`.
Evidence after fix: Smoke printed `{ "packets": 1, "decoded": 3840 }`.
Observed result after fix: The caller-side encode/decode path works with the new libopus-wasm API and preserves 3840-byte PCM output for one Discord frame.
What was not tested: Published-package install/lockfile path, because the matching libopus-wasm npm release has not been published yet.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
